name: Reverse Lunge
instructions: Stand with feet together. Step backward with one leg, landing on the ball of the foot and lowering the back knee toward the floor. Both knees should bend at about 90°. Keep your torso upright. Push through the front heel to return to standing. Alternate legs.
form_cues: Front knee stays over ankle, not past toes
Back knee nearly touches the floor
Push through front heel, not your toes
safety_notes: Reverse lunges are often more knee‑friendly than forward lunges because the front knee is more stable. If you feel knee discomfort, reduce depth. Use a wall or pole for balance if needed.
common_mistakes: Leaning the torso too far forward
Back knee crashing to the floor
